what_it_does: Acceptance test script for the agent-session registry. It creates temporary stores and synthetic catalog JSONL fixtures, then verifies that session:// addresses resolve to records, that FsStore whole-record primitives live under agent_sessions/ and refuse missing ids, that the Suite registry fold projects identity from records and trajectory from AGENT_SESSION_OPS events, that the agent_sessions_importer applies its exact title fallback chain with idempotency and live-record guard, and that supervisor handle/uuid canonical-id aliasing and fork semantics behave as specified (including an R2.5 regression guard).
observations: Self-contained script using tempfile, synthetic JSONL fixtures, and a global PASS/check() harness rather than pytest.
Imports contracts.address, store.fs_store, runtime.suite/registry/cognition, and loads ops/agent_sessions_importer.py via importlib.util.
Defines fixture builders _user, _asst, and _jsonl to synthesize catalog conversation files.
Verifies session:// scheme registration and resolve_address fail-loud behavior for unknown ids.
Tests FsStore save_agent_session/load/list/mtimes and refuses records with missing or empty ids.

Exercises the registry fold: records seed identity, events own state/last_activity, record re-writes refresh only identity, fresh Suite converges from disk, unknown state filters raise, malformed events are counted in fold_errors.

Tests importer title chain: last-occurrence ai-title beats last-prompt, last-prompt beats first real user turn, first real user turn is truncated to <=100 chars, summarizer one-shots fall back to untitled-envelope.
Tests importer sidechain skipping of agent-*, summarizer marking, coverage stats, idempotent re-runs, and live-record guard for non-closed records.

Tests canonical-id rule: WAKE resume=uuid folds onto catalog row; new spawn migrates handle row to first claude_session_id; registered self-announce without prior row is unsupervised-live; consult fork with resume=original and fork=true stays separate.
Includes an explicit R2.5 regression check that a supervisor-owned spawn stays supervised-live through a first-init registered event.
standouts: The fold is designed as log-is-the-index: records provide identity, the event log owns trajectory.
The importer title fallback chain is specified with tail-scan semantics and deterministic synthetic fixtures.
The supervisor handle/uuid aliasing and fork-isolation rules are acceptance-tested, including a regression guard for registered events.
conventions: Uses a custom check()/refused() harness with global PASS counter and SystemExit on failure.
Uses sys.path insertion and importlib.util to load the importer module from ops/.
Heavy docstring and inline comments encode design rationale alongside assertions.
Combines multiple assertions into single check labels for brevity.
Uses synthetic fixtures and temp directories to avoid live model/process dependencies.
concerns: The custom check harness raises SystemExit, which is not pytest-friendly and limits integration with standard test runners.
Multiple assertions per check reduce diagnostic precision when a check fails.
Imports the importer via importlib.util instead of a normal package import, which can obscure dependency issues.
Hard-coded timestamps and magic ids make the test less maintainable if event schemas change.
The file is long and mixes many scenarios, so a single failure stops the entire acceptance run.
notes: A dense, self-running acceptance contract for the agent-session registry, fold semantics, importer chain, and supervisor session identity rules.
questions: What is the exact membership and transition table of AGENT_SESSION_OPS? It is referenced but not defined here.
How does the fold order events and resolve conflicts when multiple events have the same timestamp?
What is the intended test runner — is this meant to be executed directly, via pytest, or via a custom harness?
How does the importer behave on the real >=1,000-record catalog mentioned as a separate lane?
What other schemes does resolve_address support, and how does session:// interact with them?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose in the module docstring matches the file content: it is an acceptance test covering scheme resolution, store primitives, registry fold, importer semantics, and supervisor canonical-id behavior.
apparent_themes: Deterministic acceptance testing with synthetic fixtures
Idempotency and live-record guard for backfill
Supervisor handle/uuid aliasing and fork isolation
intention_signals: The file appears to be an executable design contract: it encodes the registry's expected behavior, importer heuristics, and supervisor session-identity rules so that regressions in fold semantics, title resolution, or canonical-id handling are caught deterministically.
novelty: The explicit acceptance of supervisor handle-to-uuid migration, consult-fork isolation, and the R2.5 regression guard that a registered event must not downgrade a supervised-live spawn.
contribution: A deterministic, self-contained acceptance test that documents and enforces the agent-session registry contract: session:// resolution, whole-record store primitives, event-driven fold semantics, importer title fallback chain, idempotent backfill with live-record guard, and supervisor handle/uuid aliasing and fork rules.
summary_for_embedding: Acceptance test for the agent-session registry that verifies session:// addressing, FsStore primitives, event-driven fold projection, importer title fallback chain, idempotency, live-record guard, and supervisor handle/uuid canonical-id aliasing including fork isolation and an R2.5 regression guard.
intention_for_embedding: To serve as an executable contract and regression suite for the registry's identity/trajectory separation, importer heuristics, and supervisor session identity rules.

symbols.does: Asserts a boolean condition, prints ok/XX, increments PASS, and exits on failure.
symbols.description: The core test oracle used throughout the file. It prints a status line and raises SystemExit with a failure label if the condition is false, otherwise increments the global PASS counter.

symbols.does: Asserts that a callable raises a specified exception.
symbols.description: Helper for fail-loud checks. It invokes the provided function and verifies it raises the expected exception (default ValueError), using check() to report the result.

symbols.does: Constructs a Suite over a temp FsStore and discovered nodes.
symbols.description: Factory that creates a runtime Suite instance backed by the given store root and the nodes directory, used to exercise the registry fold projection.

symbols.does: Writes a list of records as JSON Lines to a file.
symbols.description: Fixture helper that serializes each dict in lines to JSON and writes one per line, used to build synthetic catalog conversation files.
Returns a dict representing a user message event with timestamp, cwd, git branch, version, and optional extra fields; used to construct importer fixture JSONL files.
Returns a dict representing an assistant message event with timestamp, cwd, and version; used to construct importer fixture JSONL files.
